stem: The CPU contains several components that work together when an instruction is executed.
model_answer: The Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) carries out arithmetic operations such as addition and subtraction and logical operations such as AND, OR and comparisons. Results are written to a register (often the accumulator) so that following instructions can use them.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Look for: arithmetic operations named, logic operations named, output to a register/accumulator. Mentioning 'fetches instructions' or 'decodes' is not creditable here — that is the control unit / fetch-execute cycle, not the ALU.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe the role of the ALU in the CPU.

mark_points.text: performs logical operations (e.g. AND, OR, comparisons / Boolean tests)
stores the result in a register / accumulator

misconceptions.label: ALU fetches instructions
misconceptions.description: Fetching and decoding are control-unit jobs. The ALU only operates on values the control unit hands it.
